novel_features.name: Site health snapshot
novel_features.description: One scored summary of a site's Bing health: traffic, crawl stats, top crawl issues, inbound links, and URL-submission quota — in a single command.
novel_features.rationale: No single Bing Webmaster API call returns this; it joins five endpoints and decodes the opaque crawl-issue bitflags into the verdict.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this first when an agent needs a one-shot read on how a site is doing in Bing, instead of chaining five raw endpoint calls.
novel_features.group: Composite views over the Bing API

novel_features.name: All-sites triage board
novel_features.description: Ranks every verified site in your account worst-first by crawl-issue count and traffic, one row per site.
novel_features.rationale: There is no cross-site endpoint — this is the account-wide 'what needs attention' view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For agencies or anyone with many sites: one call answers 'which of my sites needs attention' without a login per site.
novel_features.group: Composite views over the Bing API

novel_features.name: CTR-opportunity finder
novel_features.description: Surfaces queries with high impressions but low click-through, ranked by estimated lost clicks.
novel_features.rationale: The Bing API returns raw query stats; the expected-CTR curve and the lost-click ranking are computed by the CLI.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Points an agent straight at the title/meta-description rewrites most likely to recover Bing clicks.

novel_features.name: Keyword cannibalization detector
novel_features.description: Finds queries where two or more of your own pages both rank in Bing, ranked by the impression cost of the split.
novel_features.rationale: Requires grouping the query×page rows by query — no single Bing endpoint returns pages-per-query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Run before briefing new content so an agent doesn't create a page that competes with one that already ranks.

novel_features.name: Quota-aware batch submit
novel_features.description: Checks the remaining daily URL-submission quota, submits only up to that allowance, records every submission to the local store with a timestamp, and exits non-zero if any URL was dropped.
novel_features.rationale: Wraps the quota endpoint plus batch submit plus a local ledger plus a typed exit code — the quota arithmetic and the dropped-URL CI signal don't exist anywhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Safe to wire into a deploy pipeline: it never over-submits and the exit code tells CI when the day's quota ran out.

novel_features.name: Crawl-issue triage
novel_features.description: Decodes Bing's crawl-issue bitflags into plain-English categories, groups by category, and joins to page traffic so high-traffic broken pages rank first.
novel_features.rationale: GetCrawlIssues returns opaque flags; the decoding plus traffic-weighting plus grouping is net-new on top of the raw endpoint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Tells an agent which crawl errors actually matter — the ones on pages people land on — instead of a flat error dump.
novel_features.group: Composite views over the Bing API

narrative.headline: Every Bing Webmaster Tools API method, plus a local store, offline search, and SEO analytics no other Bing tool has — built agent-native.
narrative.value_prop: Bing Webmaster Tools has no official spec and no first-party CLI. This wraps the whole documented API surface — sites, sitemaps, URL and content submission, traffic and query stats, crawl control, inbound links, keyword research, and per-site config — then adds analytics commands the API itself doesn't expose: `sites health` (a scored five-endpoint summary), `sites triage` (worst-first across every site), `traffic ctr-gaps` (high-impression / low-CTR queries by estimated lost clicks), `keywords cannibalization` (queries where two of your pages compete), `crawl triage` (decoded crawl issues weighted by traffic), and `submit smart` (quota-aware batch submission with a local ledger and a CI exit code). Synced data (`sync`) and submission history land in a local SQLite store you can query with `search` and `sql`.
narrative.auth_narrative: Auth is a single API key — open the Bing Webmaster Tools dashboard, go to Settings → API access, generate a key, and set `BING_WEBMASTER_API_KEY`. The key is passed as the `apikey` query parameter on every call. Run `doctor` to confirm it is valid and the API is reachable.

quickstart.comment: Confirm BING_WEBMASTER_API_KEY is set and ssl.bing.com is reachable.
List the sites in your account — you need the site URL for everything below.
One scored summary: traffic, crawl, top issues, links, quota.
Find the high-impression / low-CTR queries worth a title rewrite.
Is this URL indexed by Bing — and if not, what's the crawl reason?
Submit changed URLs for indexing without blowing the daily quota.

troubleshoots.symptom: `ERROR!!! InvalidApiKey` or HTTP 400 on every call
troubleshoots.fix: Your apikey is wrong or missing — copy it again from the dashboard's Settings → API access page and re-export BING_WEBMASTER_API_KEY.

troubleshoots.symptom: `submit url` fails with a quota error
troubleshoots.fix: Run `submit quota --site-url <site>` to see the remaining daily allowance; new accounts often get ~10/day. Use `submit smart` to stay under it, or switch real-time submission to IndexNow.

Run `sync` first — those commands read the local store, which starts empty. Note that stat resources require a site URL, so `sync` only populates the account-wide tables (sites) plus anything `submit smart` has recorded.
The CLI normalizes Bing's legacy WCF dates to ISO-8601 in its output; if you see the raw form you're reading ssl.bing.com directly rather than through this CLI.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI when an agent or operator needs to read or manage a site's presence in Bing search — pulling query and page performance, checking whether URLs are indexed and why, submitting URLs or sitemaps within quota, reviewing and triaging crawl errors, doing Bing keyword research, or auditing per-site crawl/blocking/geo config. Prefer it over a raw HTTP call when you want the decoded crawl-issue categories, the analytics commands, or a JSON shape that's already unwrapped from Bing's `{"d":...}` envelope.

recipes.title: Site health in one call
recipes.explanation: Joins traffic, crawl stats, crawl issues, link counts, and submission quota into one scored report.

recipes.title: Find Bing CTR opportunities
recipes.explanation: Lists queries getting impressions but few clicks, ranked by estimated lost clicks — your title-rewrite worklist.

recipes.title: Is this URL indexed and why not
recipes.explanation: Joins GetUrlInfo with GetCrawlIssues and decodes the issue flags into a plain-English reason.

recipes.title: Catch keyword cannibalization with a narrow payload
recipes.explanation: Finds queries where two of your pages both rank; the dotted --select keeps the agent payload to just the fields that matter.

recipes.title: Post-deploy index push for CI
recipes.explanation: Submits the changed URLs up to the remaining daily quota; non-zero exit if any were dropped, so the pipeline flags it.

trigger_phrases: check bing search performance for my site
is this url indexed by bing
submit urls to bing for indexing
